canonical_source_description: Repository markdown files are the canonical documentation source. Wiki pages are derived publishing surfaces and must stay aligned with the repository markdown they summarize.

sync_pairs.description: Root constitution and derived wiki constitution page.
Root agent operating rules and derived role overview.
Root agent operating rules and derived workflow page.
Architect role definition and derived role overview.
Builder role definition and derived role overview.
Documentation Manager role definition and derived role overview.
Release Manager role definition and derived role overview.
Validator role definition and derived role overview.
Compliance policy and derived wiki compliance page.
Change control policy and derived workflow page.
Documentation policy and derived documentation page.
Release policy and derived releases page.
Repository README and derived overview page.
Repository README and wiki home navigation page.
Project changelog and derived wiki changelog page.
Repository vision and wiki entry-point summary.
Contributor guidance and derived workflow page.
Documentation standard and derived workflow page.
Review standard and derived workflow page.
Versioning standard and derived releases page.
Changelog standard and derived changelog page.
Machine-readable role policy and derived role overview.
Machine-readable compliance rules and derived compliance page.
Machine-readable versioning rules and derived releases page.
Machine-readable changelog rules and derived changelog page.

timing.same_pr_sync: When a canonical source is modified, the corresponding derived surface must be updated in the same pull request unless a docs:needs-sync label or linked follow-up task contract is explicitly used.
timing.follow_up_tolerance: Follow-up sync work must be traceable and completed under DOCUMENTATION_POLICY.md timing requirements.
timing.maximum_drift_age: Drift older than the tolerance defined in DOCUMENTATION_POLICY.md is a compliance violation.

tolerance.formatting_differences: Minor formatting differences are acceptable when they do not alter meaning. Wiki-specific navigation is permitted.
tolerance.content_divergence: Derived pages must not contradict their canonical sources.
tolerance.addition_policy: Derived surfaces may add navigation and cross-references but must not introduce new binding requirements.

format.json_to_markdown_rendering: When a canonical source is JSON, the derived markdown must summarize current fields, values, and relationships without contradiction.

review_scope: Verify modified canonical sources have corresponding derived surface updates or documented follow-up tracking.
Verify derived wiki pages do not contradict canonical sources.
Verify wiki-specific additions are limited to navigation, orientation, and cross-reference aids.
Verify JSON-to-markdown derived documents accurately summarize the current JSON canonical source.
Flag stale or missing paths as documentation drift.
review_requirements.review_timing: Documentation sync review must occur before the validator renders a final compliance decision on any change that modifies canonical sources.
review_requirements.escalation: If widespread drift is detected across multiple sync pairs, the documentation_manager must escalate to the architect for a dedicated documentation sync task contract.
